export_view.value:
Automated Notifications help create a more organized and customer-friendly business environment by alerting customers or staff members when changes are made in RepairQ. This automation streamlines communication, reducing the time it takes to notify customers and allowing for more efficient interactions between teams. Ultimately, this enhances the overall customer experience.

Admins and Managers can create automated notifications tailored to the specific needs of the business. Common examples of notifications include:

  • Notify customers when their device is in ‘Ready for Pickup’ status.

    • To ensure successful delivery, the customer’s profile must have a valid SMS phone number, carrier, and approval to receive SMS messages.

  • Send a follow-up link to an experience survey after a ticket is closed.

  • Notify Managers when Purchase Orders have been created.

  • Alert Managers when inventory items have been placed in a specific status, such as Damaged, Shrinkage, Pending RMA, etc.

Important: Automated Notifications are for transactional and relationship messaging only.

This feature is designed to send ticket status updates, appointment reminders, pickup notifications, internal alerts, and similar relationship-based communications. It is not intended, and must not be used, for promotional, marketing, or advertising content (including sales, discounts, loyalty offers, or new product announcements).

Using Automated Notifications to send marketing material may violate anti-spam and consumer protection laws, including but not limited to:

  • The CAN-SPAM Act (United States)

  • The Telephone Consumer Protection Act, or TCPA (United States SMS)

  • Canada's Anti-Spam Legislation, or CASL

  • The UK's Privacy and Electronic Communications Regulations (PECR)

  • The EU's GDPR and ePrivacy Directive

  • Australia's Spam Act 2003

  • Specified Electronic Mail Act (Japan)

  • Similar regulations in other jurisdictions

You are responsible for ensuring your use of this feature complies with all laws that apply to you and your customers. If you want to send promotional or marketing content, use a dedicated marketing platform that supports consent tracking, opt-outs, and sender identification requirements. See our Third-Party Marketing Consent article for guidance on capturing marketing consent, and consult legal counsel if you are unsure what applies to your business.

SMS messaging may carry additional restrictions. In the United States, the TCPA requires prior express written consent for marketing texts and carries statutory damages of $500 to $1,500 per violation. Carriers and messaging providers (including Twilio) independently enforce policies against promotional content on transactional messaging routes, and may suspend or terminate sending without notice if violations are detected.

Additional Resources

  • Automated Notification Resources

  • Getting Started: The RepairQ Notification Queue

  • Automated Notifications: Inventory Status Changes

  • Sending an SMS Notification to a Customer

Viewing Automated Notifications

On the Navigation Bar, go to Settings → Company Settings. On the Settings Overview page, the left column lists settings by category. Click the Communications dropdown. From the list of available settings, click Notifications.

Note: You can add notifications at the location level by going to Settings → Location Settings.

Creating an Automated Notification

On the Notification Overview page, click Create Notification Automation.

Fill out the fields to modify your notifications, triggers, and timing. You can customize these to fit your needs based on the purpose of the notification. Some of the fields can use shortcodes to insert dynamic data from RepairQ.

  • Notification Name - Name the notification to indicate the purpose for easy identification.

  • Location - Select whether this notification is assigned to all locations or a specific location.

  • Trigger - This indicates the prompt that will create the notification. Some triggers can create additional actions for customization. These are generally status changes or manual notifications.

  • Delivery - Select the delivery method for the notification. These can be sent via Email, SMS, or both.

  • Message Subject - This is the subject/title of your outgoing notification.

  • Send To - Select an email address or use the provided shortcodes to send the message to a recipient. Multiple recipients can be added, separated by commas.

  • Reply To - (optional) Select the delivery address/shortcode for who will receive any responses to the notification. If left blank, this will default to your reply-to address in RepairQ.

  • Message - This is the body of your notification. You can use any relevant shortcodes to populate information into your notification when it is generated.

    • When composing an email message, you can add some basic HTML formatting using the WYSIWYG editor.

    • SMS messages longer than 160 characters will be split into multiple messages. SMS does not support HTML or any special formatting.

    • If you select email + SMS, you must fill out both message fields. This allows you to create different messages for each delivery method. 

  • Attachment - Some triggers will allow you to send a .pdf attachment of a receipt or invoice. The information on this attachment can be configured in the Email Receipts Template.

  • Send Delay - Select a time frame if you wish to delay the delivery time of the notification. This can be measured in Minutes, Hours, or Days. Decimals can be used for fractional amounts.

  • Active - Select this to make sure your notification is active. Notifications that are not marked Active are disabled until reactivated.

After you have made your selections, be sure to Save the automation. You will be directed back to the Notification Overview page, where you can view and edit your existing automation

Shortcodes

Shortcodes allow you to personalize your notification messages by dynamically customizing your notification messages. Copy and paste the {bracketed fields} into the recipient and communication fields where you want the information added to your message. When the notification is created, these fields will be replaced with the appropriate data based on the notification context.

Note: Applicable shortcodes are listed above each field. Information will not populate to bracketed fields not listed in the available selection.

Notification Queue

On the Navigation Bar, go to Reports → Notifications to view your Notification Queue. When an Automated Notification is triggered, the outgoing message will appear in the list. You can view and filter additional details, and cancel the delivery of a pending communication.

START 1.21.X
